subject: Social Science (CBSE - Social and Political Life – II)

activities_projects.title: Gender Role Analysis Survey
activities_projects.objective: Analyze gender roles in your community
steps: Prepare questions about who does which tasks at home or school
Conduct a survey among 20 households
Record responses and categorize tasks by gender
Write a short analysis comparing results with chapter content
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students critically assess traditional gender roles
activities_projects.evaluation: Survey design, data accuracy, and analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Research on Women Leaders
activities_projects.objective: Explore contributions of women leaders globally and in India
steps: Choose 3 women leaders (social, political, or educational)
Research their life, struggles, and achievements
Prepare a comparison table showing similarities and differences
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand diverse paths to social change
activities_projects.evaluation: Research depth, clarity, and comparison (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Class Debate: Education for Girls
activities_projects.objective: Discuss importance and challenges of girls’ education
steps: Divide class into affirmative and opposing teams
Use chapter examples to argue your points
Conclude with a class vote on key takeaways
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop argumentation and critical thinking
activities_projects.evaluation: Content accuracy, participation, and reasoning (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Case Study: Women’s Movements
activities_projects.objective: Examine historic and modern movements for women’s rights
steps: Choose one movement mentioned in the chapter
Explain its causes, events, and impact
Present findings in a 2–3 minute class presentation
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students connect history with current social issues
activities_projects.evaluation: Content accuracy, presentation clarity, and engagement (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Poster on Breaking Stereotypes
steps: Select at least 5 examples from different fields (science, politics, sports, arts)
Create a poster showing women achieving success
Add slogans emphasizing equality
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity, relevance, and visual appeal (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Interview Project: Women in Professions
activities_projects.objective: Understand experiences of women in different professions
steps: Interview 3 working women in your community
Ask about challenges, achievements, and advice for girls
Summarize findings and share in class discussion
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students link real-life experiences with chapter concepts
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of questions, analysis, and presentation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Creative Story: A Day in the Life of a Woman Leader
activities_projects.objective: Develop empathy and understanding through writing
steps: Imagine being a woman leader in your town or school
Describe daily challenges, achievements, and interactions
Highlight lessons learned about equality and perseverance
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students practice reflective writing and empathy

activities_projects.title: Campaign Plan: Gender Equality
steps: Select one issue (education, employment, domestic work, etc.)
Plan steps for raising awareness in school or community
Create a poster or pamphlet as part of your campaign
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand civic responsibility and advocacy

activities_projects.title: Compare Past and Present Women’s Roles
activities_projects.objective: Analyze how opportunities for women have changed
steps: Make a timeline of women’s roles from early 20th century to today
Include education, professions, and political participation
Write 5 observations and discuss in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop historical perspective and analytical skills

activities_projects.title: Mini Quiz: Women Change the World
activities_projects.objective: Test understanding of key concepts and events
steps: Prepare 15 medium-level questions from the chapter
Conduct a quiz in class in teams
Discuss answers and clarify misconceptions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students reinforce knowledge and critical thinking
activities_projects.evaluation: Question relevance, participation, and discussion (10 marks)
